description_long: This object is a medium-sized, angular stone with a faceted, geometric appearance, featuring multiple flat surfaces and sharp edges. Its surface is uniformly gray, lacking any visible veining, mottling, or color variation, giving it a monolithic and somewhat artificial look. The stone has a blocky, irregular shape, with some faces appearing taller and more vertical, while others are sloped or gently inclined. The overall texture appears matte and rough, suggesting a natural, unpolished finish typical of quarried or broken stone. There are no visible signs of significant weathering, cracks, or biological growth, indicating it is relatively new or has been recently placed. This type of stone could serve as a barrier or obstacle in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A medium-sized, angular gray stone with a faceted, blocky shape and a rough, matte surface. It appears unweathered and monolithic, suitable as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears squat and wide, with several flat, angular faces and a low, broad profile.
description_view_1: This view shows the stone as taller and more vertical, with a prominent peak and sharply defined edges.
description_view_2: The stone looks broad and blocky, with a slightly sloped top and multiple intersecting flat surfaces.
description_view_3: From this angle, the stone appears tall and narrow, with deep vertical facets and a pronounced angularity.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surface is uniformly colored and faceted with geometric planes.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears unweathered and freshly cut, with no cracks, erosion, or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made structures but can blend with other landscaping stones or urban greenery when placed in parks or sidewalks.
